reasoning: The baseline Polymarket price on January 22, 2026, was 66%, reflecting strong market expectation that Bitcoin would drop to $75,000 by year-end. Since then, the price has experienced a sharp decline. By late January, Bitcoin fell from the ~$90,000 range, and on January 31, 2026, amid geopolitical tensions and thin weekend liquidity, it suffered a major collapse. Reports indicate the price dropped below $78,000, falling 'temporarily to nearly $75,000'. With the price now within only a few percentage points of the $75,000 threshold and 11 full months still remaining in 2026, the probability of a single 1-minute candle wicking down to $75,000 or lower on Binance is extremely high. Even if the target wasn't hit on January 31, standard crypto volatility over the extended timeframe makes it highly probable it will occur before Dec 31, 2026. Therefore, I'm updating significantly upward from the baseline to 89%.
